pubmed.title: Pancreatic Cancer: Changing Epidemiology and New Approaches to Risk Assessment, Early Detection, and Prevention.
Advances in the epidemiology of pancreatic cancer: Trends, risk factors, screening, and prognosis.

open_targets.name: pancreatic neoplasm
open_targets.description: Neoplasm located in the pancreas. Depending on the types of ISLET CELLS present in the tumors, various hormones can be secreted: GLUCAGON from PANCREATIC ALPHA CELLS; INSULIN from PANCREATIC BETA CELLS; and SOMATOSTATIN from the SOMATOSTATIN-SECRETING CELLS. Most are malignant except the insulin-producing tumors (INSULINOMA).

open_targets.name: pancreatic carcinoma
open_targets.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the exocrine pancreas

open_targets.name: Malignant Pancreatic Neoplasm
open_targets.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the pancreas.

open_targets.name: Neoplasm of the pancreas
open_targets.description: A tumor (abnormal growth of tissue) of the pancreas.

open_targets.name: pancreatic cancer, susceptibility to, 4
open_targets.description: Any familial pancreatic carcinoma in which the cause of the disease is a mutation in the BRCA1 gene.

hypotheses.title: Dietary methionine restriction may sensitize KRAS-driven pancreatic tumors to targeted metabolic therapy
hypotheses.mechanism: Limiting methionine reduces one‑carbon/ SAM levels, dampening KRAS‑driven protein methylation and downstream signaling, which can increase tumor cell apoptosis
hypotheses.rationale: KRAS mutations are present in >90% of pancreatic cancers (pubmed:38686056) and rely on heightened methyltransferase activity (pubmed:34216688); short‑term methionine restriction is tolerable in early human studies (pubmed:26320426)
hypotheses.next_test: Phase I trial of a 7‑day low‑methionine diet combined with standard chemotherapy in patients with resectable KRAS‑mutant tumors

hypotheses.title: Gut microbiota‑derived short‑chain fatty acids could modulate KRAS signaling and tumor inflammation
hypotheses.mechanism: SCFAs such as butyrate inhibit histone deacetylases, potentially reversing KRAS‑induced transcriptional programs and suppressing pro‑inflammatory cytokine release
hypotheses.rationale: Elevated inflammatory signatures are linked to poorer outcomes (pubmed:36804602); epidemiological studies show diet‑microbiome interactions affect pancreatic cancer risk (pubmed:21620466)
hypotheses.next_test: Pre‑clinical model testing butyrate supplementation combined with KRAS inhibitors in mouse xenografts

hypotheses.title: Targeting mucin O‑glycosylation pathways may limit pancreatic cancer cell adhesion and metastasis
hypotheses.mechanism: Inhibition of the GalNAc‑transferases that initiate O‑GPAS glycosylation reduces tumor cell motility and EGFR‑driven proliferation
hypotheses.rationale: Mucinous histology shows high expression of O‑linked glycans (pubmed:26320426); experimental inhibitors have shown anti‑tumor activity in vitro (pubmed:34216688)
hypotheses.next_test: Phase II pilot of a GalNAc‑transferase inhibitor in patients with mucinous pancreatic adenocarcinoma

hypotheses.title: NAD+ supplementation could re‑wire metabolic dependencies in early‑stage pancreatic lesions
hypotheses.mechanism: Elevated NAD+ levels enhance sirtuin activity, promoting DNA repair and potentially counteracting metabolic stress in precancerous PanIN cells
hypotheses.rationale: PanIN lesions exhibit altered nucleotide metabolism (pubmed:26320426); pre‑clinical data suggest NAD+ precursors reduce tumor growth (pubmed:38686056)
hypotheses.next_test: Ex vivo organoid assay evaluating nicotinamide riboside treatment on PanIN‑derived organoids with KRAS mutation

hypotheses.title: BRCA1/2 mutation carriers may benefit from prophylactic PARP inhibition combined with lifestyle modification
hypotheses.mechanism: PARP inhibition exploits synthetic lethality in HR‑deficient pancreatic epithelium; concurrent weight management may reduce insulin‑driven DNA damage accumulation
hypotheses.rationale: Familial pancreatic cancer linked to BRCA mutations (open_targets:MONDO_0013685); PARP inhibitors have shown efficacy in metastatic disease (pubmed:38686056)
hypotheses.next_test: Observational cohort study of PARP‑inhibitor use in BRCA‑positive familial pancreatic cancer registries

hypotheses.title: Bile acid metabolites could serve as early detection biomarkers when combined with dietary fiber intake
hypotheses.mechanism: Specific secondary bile acids up‑regulate FXR signaling, which has anti‑inflammatory effects and may suppress tumor progression
hypotheses.rationale: Serum bile acid profiles differ in pancreatic cancer patients (pubmed:36804602); high‑fiber diets increase bile acid biotransformation (pubmed:21620466)
hypotheses.next_test: Case‑control study measuring serum bile acid signatures and dietary fiber records in at‑risk individuals

hypotheses.title: Stroma‑targeted TGF‑β inhibition combined with selenium supplementation may improve drug delivery
hypotheses.mechanism: TGF‑β blockade remodels the dense pancreatic stromal matrix, enhancing vascular perfusion; selenium may act as an antioxidant to protect normal tissue during remodeling
hypotheses.rationale: Stromal fibrosis limits chemotherapy penetration (pubmed:34216688); selenium supplementation has been explored for anti‑fibrotic effects in other cancers (pubmed:26320426)
hypotheses.next_test: Pre‑clinical study of a TGF‑β neutralizing antibody plus selenium diet in orthotopic pancreatic tumor models

hypotheses.title: MicroRNA‑based nutraceuticals targeting KRAS‑regulated exosomal cargo could modulate tumor‑microenvironment signaling
hypotheses.mechanism: Engineered plant‑derived exosomes delivering tumor‑suppressor miRNAs aim to reprogram cancer‑associated fibroblasts to a less supportive state
hypotheses.rationale: Exosomal miRNA dysregulation is implicated in pancreatic cancer communication (pubmed:26320426); dietary compounds such as curcumin influence miRNA metabolism (pubmed:38686056)
hypotheses.next_test: In vitro validation of curcumin‑treated exosomal miR‑34a delivery on pancreatic cancer cell co‑culture with fibroblasts

hypotheses.title: Metabolomic profiling of circulating ketone bodies may identify a subset of patients who respond to ketogenic diet adjunct therapy
hypotheses.mechanism: Ketogenic diet reduces glucose availability, forcing tumor cells to rely on fatty acid oxidation; elevated ketones may induce metabolic stress selectively in KRAS‑mutant cells
hypotheses.rationale: Ketogenic interventions have shown modest anti‑inflammatory effects and are being tested in oncology (pubmed:36804602); pancreatic cancers exhibit altered substrate utilization (pubmed:34216688)
hypotheses.next_test: Pilot feasibility trial of a 4‑week therapeutic ketogenic diet with urinary ketone monitoring in newly diagnosed pancreatic cancer patients

hypotheses.title: Epigenetic priming using dietary betaine could enhance response to KRAS‑directed synthetic lethal drugs
hypotheses.mechanism: Betaine donates methyl groups, supporting DNA methylation patterns that may sensitize KRAS‑mutant cells to inhibitors of downstream effectors
hypotheses.rationale: Methyl donor deficiency is linked to genomic instability (pubmed:21620466); synthetic lethal screens have identified KRAS‑dependent vulnerabilities that could be accentuated by epigenetic modulation (pubmed:38686056)
hypotheses.next_test: In vivo study of betaine supplementation combined with a KRAS‑G12C inhibitor in mouse models of pancreatic cancer
